chunk_summary: The maternal-fetal risk checklist identifies intergenerational factors contributing to stunting, emphasizing maternal health, anemia, and nutritional interventions, with program linkages to AMB, WIFS, and dietary guidelines for prevention.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of maternal-fetal factors in early growth faltering and stunting.
Learn how to use the maternal-fetal risk checklist to identify and address stunting determinants.
Recognize the importance of linking mothers and adolescent girls to nutritional programs like AMB and WIFS.
Promote dietary diversity and supplementation during pregnancy and lactation to prevent stunting.
key_facts: Maternal anemia and poor antenatal weight gain are key risk factors for fetal growth restriction and stunting.
Small-for-gestational-age status and poor early weight gain predict later stunting risk.
Iron–folic acid supplementation is recommended for mothers and adolescent girls (e.g., WIFS and AMB programs).
Calcium supplementation during pregnancy and lactation should follow national guidelines.
Dietary diversity should align with ICMR-NIN food-group targets to improve maternal and child nutrition.
reference_quote: Ensure mothers and adolescent girls in the household are connected to iron–folic acid supplementation platforms (including AMB and WIFS where applicable).
chunk_heading: STUNTING, CHRONIC UNDERNUTRITION & DETERMINANTS;MATERNAL FETAL RISK CHECKLIST AND PROGRAM LINKAGE IN STUNTING VISITS;
subtopic: MATERNAL FETAL RISK CHECKLIST AND PROGRAM LINKAGE IN STUNTING VISITS
